computation_name: DTW Lower-Bound Pruning and Early Abandoning
definition: Use cheap lower bounds, envelopes, early abandoning, and progressive filtering to discard train series that cannot be among the nearest neighbors before computing full dynamic time warping distance.

scale_profile.best_for: Time-series similarity where exact DTW is scientifically desired but too expensive over all pairs.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Large time-series collections when lower bounds are tight and candidate pruning is strong.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The distance is not lower-boundable or all series are so similar that pruning rarely triggers.

tunable_parameters.name: warping_window
tunable_parameters.purpose: Constrains alignments and affects both speed and scientific meaning.

tunable_parameters.name: lower_bound_type
tunable_parameters.purpose: Selects LB_Keogh, improved bounds, or another valid lower bound.

tunable_parameters.name: top_k
tunable_parameters.purpose: Number of nearest train series retained.

quality_gates: Verify the lower bound never exceeds exact DTW on sampled pairs.
Report warping constraints and similarity transform from distance to similarity.
Compare pruned top-k results with exact DTW on a small subset.
failure_modes: Loose lower bounds may provide little speedup.
Overly tight warping constraints can change the scientific distance definition.
Distance-to-similarity scaling can affect SPECTRA thresholds.
example_usage: Use the dense-vector script only as an output-contract reference; production DTW search should emit the same columns after lower-bound pruning and exact DTW reranking.

citations.title: A new lower bound for dynamic time warping
citations.evidence: Describes DTW as quadratic and uses lower bounds to avoid unnecessary full DTW computations.

citations.title: PrunedDTW: Time series dynamic time warping faster by pruning
citations.evidence: Studies early abandoning and pruning for faster DTW computation.
